feedback.text: I would kindly like the Digital Fairness act to address the issue of digital video game publishers ending support and revoking access to products they have already sold, without notice to customers prior to purchase nor an end-of-life plan. It has become common for publishers to revoke access from players who have already bought and paid for something that is marketed as a product but treated as a service. The presence of clear legislation on this matter would greatly help defend the rights of consumers in an industry where those rights are much more vague and prone to being exploited by publishers. As a non-EU citizen I also hope that any legislation passed that is fair and for the protection on consumers can set an precedent and example for non-EU countries.
